title: Sonnet 75 by Edmund Spenser — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
One day I wrote her name upon the strand,
But came the waves and washed it away:
Agayne I wrote it with a second hand,
But came the tyde, and made my paynes his pray.
"Vayne man," sayd she, "that doest in vaine assay.
A mortall thing so to immortalize,
For I my selve shall lyke to this decay,
and eek my name bee wyped out lykewize."
meaning: "Sonnet 75" by Edmund Spenser is a sad poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of sad through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "One day I wrote her name upon the strand, But came the waves and washed it away:...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edmund Spenser's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
